governing_requirement: Freeze one deterministic cross-project identity tuple across execution, encoded/decoded media, time, scene/character/person assignment, package revision, intent, and mask artifact identities; collisions, drift, ambiguity, reused indices, duplicate intents, and character-revision mismatches fail closed.
existing_enforcement: The frozen request and receipt schemas require project/run/job/pass/attempt/hypothesis identity, encoded and canonical decoded source identity, still/video/frame/span identity, scene/shot/take, character revision, canonical scene instance, canonical and provider-local person indices, assignment evidence, intent IDs, package lineage, and mask artifact identities.

Request validation checks canonical payload/signature/trust, media/source closure, owner roster uniqueness, target/protected ownership, artifact identity hashes, source/transform binding, within-request intent uniqueness, and exact Mode A/refinement identities.

Exchange validation binds request to receipt across direct execution fields, media, source, subject, assignment-evidence hash, release, transform, input lineage, exactly one output per intent, output owner, package lineage, resource envelope, and exact certificate scope.
Existing focused bridge tests prove single-exchange source/media/owner/transform/authority drift fails closed.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Replace request.subject.assignment_evidence.mapping_sha256 with an arbitrary 64-hex value and trusted re-sign the request.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: Recompute the assignment evidence identity from mapping ID, status, bbox, skeleton, silhouette, depth and normalized subject/provider binding rather than trusting mapping_sha256.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Create two separately valid trusted requests for the same scene_instance_id with different character_revision values.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: A deterministic identity-set decision must reject one scene instance mapping to multiple character revisions within the declared project/run/scene scope.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Create two distinct request IDs/idempotency keys that reuse the same intent ID in the same project/run/job/pass/attempt/hypothesis scope.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: Permit only an exact same-body idempotent replay; reject a duplicate intent identity attached to a distinct transaction or body.

confirmed_gaps.observed: No producer module or schema constructs one normalized identity tuple, recomputes every component, evaluates a record set, and emits a canonical decision hash.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: Add a separate producer decision around the frozen v1 documents.

confirmed_gaps.observed: Single requests carry provider_person_index but there is no cross-record decision proving one-to-one mapping to a canonical scene instance within exact media/provider scope.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: Reject reused provider-local indices, one scene instance mapped to multiple provider indices, and assignment drift.

confirmed_gaps.observed: The request has correlation_id but the frozen v1 receipt does not. The exchange validator therefore cannot expose one content-addressed identity that includes correlation, package, intent, media/time and artifacts without a separate decision record.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: The separate decision must bind correlation from the signed request while leaving the frozen receipt unchanged.

canceled_legacy_request.control_reason: Held after prior worktree materialization timeout; preserve and restage only after scheduler correction.
canceled_legacy_request.reason_not_restaged: Its allowed scope mutates frozen v1 request/receipt/fixture bytes and is superseded by the additive replacement design.

replacement_implementation_packet.objective: Implement a producer-owned, deterministic canonical identity and collision decision around frozen v1 request/receipt records, with no frozen schema or fixture mutation.
replacement_implementation_packet.path_state: All six allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate paths are read-only. The unit is disjoint from frozen 08.05/08.06/08.07 paths and prepared 09.02, 09.04 and 09.05 allowed paths.
acceptance_criteria: Load and self-hash one closed identity policy defining exact normalization, scope and collision reason codes.
Recompute assignment_evidence_sha256 from all normalized assignment components plus canonical subject/provider identity; an asserted mapping hash is never trusted.

Construct one canonical identity tuple covering project/run/correlation/job/pass/attempt/hypothesis, request/idempotency, encoded and decoded media identity, decoder/orientation/color, video/frame/span time, scene/shot/take, character ID/revision, canonical scene/person identity, provider-local index and assignment, package/revision, intent, owner, label/type/space, and mask artifact identity.
Emit component hashes, canonical tuple hash, policy ID/hash, allow/reject result, closed ordered reasons, normalized facts and canonical decision hash under the new strict decision schema.

Evaluate sets deterministically and reject encoded/pixel/decoder/time drift, omitted or ambiguous owners, assignment-hash drift, provider-index reuse, non-bijective person mappings, scene-instance character-revision drift, artifact collisions, and duplicate intents across distinct bodies.
Allow only exact same-body idempotent replay with identical tuple/request hashes; same key or intent with different body fails closed.
Bind Mode A package revision and selector artifacts while preserving explicit null/not-applicable package facts for Mode B.
Golden vectors cover still image, video frame/span, multi-person distinct indices, exact replay, every required collision/drift failure and record-order invariance.
No identity decision grants mask authority, training truth, use eligibility, release adoption, or cache reuse by itself.
Frozen v1 schemas, fixtures, catalog, semantic profile, validation.py, and all preserved dispatcher artifacts remain byte-identical.

observed_verification.command: python -m pytest -q tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'all_positive_documents or request_ownership_transform or receipt_authority_parent'

claim_boundary: This audit confirms the additive cross-record identity gap and prepares a replacement worker unit. It does not implement the identity decision, complete MF-P6-09.01, or authorize downstream transforms, consumption, eligibility, cache, release, or authority.
